speaker: Narrator
text: She's desperate to feed Pasaya, saving the life of the person most dear to her would be utter bliss.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Everything in the cup had liquefied. Fries, chicken, beef, bun, all of it had become one fragrant slush within the plastic vessel. 
	
	It was a grotesque sight. Christina wanted to throw up, but it's like her body didn't let her, Pasaya made it for her after all. When she sucks a portion of it in, her opinion changes immediately.
	
	All Christina could taste was tartness, like nectar, now she couldn't get enough.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She tipped the cup further and chugged it down, splattering the contents on her face and desk as much as it entered her own mouth. 
	
	She couldn't even taste stomach acid oddly, it's just the love. Pasaya's love.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Oh, I can feel the love flowing all the way down my thorax!

speaker: Pasaya
text: This will sustain us for more time than I could've possibly hoped for.

speaker: Pasaya
text: If I could only reach out and kiss you.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Isn't it time for a livestream?

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina checked Pasaya's web page. She had changed her mind and followed through on the weekend stream after all. This time for a full 12 hours of content.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Her world went silent for the first time in days. Her earbuds had been disconnected for so long that they'd already turned off. 
	
	Her phone was resting on the desk, disconnected without any kind of media running, and even the computer had been asleep for the past hour or so. 
	
	She had no idea where the voices had been coming from. She did, however, notice the vomit still dangling on her lip and the brown stains on her university coursework.
